description: Rust compile error fix test cases for LLM benchmarking. Each case represents a real error pattern from Python-to-Rust transpilation.

cases.name: E0308: type mismatch (usize -> String)
cases.explanation: items.len() returns usize but function expects String. Fix: .to_string() or format!

cases.name: E0433: unresolved module (Python os -> Rust std::env)
cases.error_message: failed to resolve: use of unresolved module `os`
cases.explanation: Python's os.environ maps to Rust's std::env::var(). os::path::join -> Path::new().join()

cases.name: E0599: PathBuf doesn't impl Display
cases.explanation: PathBuf doesn't impl Display. Use path.display() or {:?} debug format.

cases.name: E0369: PathBuf / operator (Python path join)
cases.explanation: Python's / operator for paths maps to Rust's .join() method.

cases.name: E0425: Python MagicMock in Rust test
cases.error_message: cannot find function `MagicMock` in this scope
cases.explanation: Python's MagicMock has no direct Rust equivalent. Should create a mock struct with the needed fields.

cases.name: E0308: bool used where i64 expected
cases.explanation: Python allows bool + int, Rust doesn't. Fix: `item as i64` or `if item { 1 } else { 0 }`.

cases.name: E0277: expected &str, found String
cases.explanation: HashMap::get takes a reference. Also return type needs &str not &String. Fix: map.get(&key) and Some(val.as_str())

cases.name: E0382: use of moved value
cases.explanation: data.into_iter() consumes data. Fix: use data.iter() instead, or clone, or compute len before consuming.
